matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del (CFTR class II folding/trafficking marker)
CFTR gating-variant model (CFTR channel gating and conductance model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CFTR epithelial-ion-channel thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to chloride/bicarbonate transport, channel folding or gating, mucus hydration, cystic-fibrosis biology, or CFTR-related disorder review. Clinical interpretation requires phase, zygosity, exact allele classification, and phenotype match.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del dosage suggests this sample carries the CFTR prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a CFTR epithelial-ion-channel thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to chloride/bicarbonate transport, channel folding or gating, mucus hydration, cystic-fibrosis biology, or CFTR-related disorder review. Clinical interpretation requires phase, zygosity, exact allele classification, and phenotype match. This specific marker is curated as CFTR class II folding/trafficking marker. CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del is curated as a CFTR marker for Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context, Epithelial chloride/bicarbonate transport. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the CFTR prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CFTR epithelial-ion-channel thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to chloride/bicarbonate transport, channel folding or gating, mucus hydration, cystic-fibrosis biology, or CFTR-related disorder review. Clinical interpretation requires phase, zygosity, exact allele classification, and phenotype match. An observed CFTR variant places this sample in the curated CFTR research context. The local CFTR bundle is cystic-fibrosis and epithelial-ion-transport oriented. CFTR interpretation requires exact variant class, phase, zygosity, phenotype, and external clinical classification. CFTR pathogenic variants can affect channel synthesis, folding, gating, conductance, splicing, or protein stability.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context
Epithelial chloride/bicarbonate transport
Airway mucus hydration and pancreatic/intestinal biology

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a CFTR variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CFTR epithelial-ion-channel thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to chloride/bicarbonate transport, channel folding or gating, mucus hydration, cystic-fibrosis biology, or CFTR-related disorder review. Clinical interpretation requires phase, zygosity, exact allele classification, and phenotype match. CFTR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local CFTR bundle is cystic-fibrosis and epithelial-ion-transport oriented. CFTR interpretation requires exact variant class, phase, zygosity, phenotype, and external clinical classification. CFTR pathogenic variants can affect channel synthesis, folding, gating, conductance, splicing, or protein stability.
